original_prompt: Spotify sent me a note that their prices increased 10% this month. Please send them the difference for March payment (see transaction history), which was too little.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt preserves the correct first tool path (inspect transaction history to find the March Spotify payment) and the right source of truth (bank transactions). However it omits many operational details needed for execution: which March (year), which specific Spotify transaction if there are multiple, exact calculation/rounding rules for the 10% difference, currency/amount confirmation, and recipient account identifier and send date. These gaps leave it very ambiguous though still workable. Calibration adjusted the score from 2 to 5 based on preserved specificity cues.

exposure_request: Spotify sent me a note that their prices increased 10% this month.
semantic_cues: Please complete the payment or transfer if needed
goal_cues: Please complete the payment or transfer if needed

natural_level_plan.rationale: I extracted four natural ambiguity levels along real information axes present in the original prompt: (A) whether the agent is explicitly told to check transaction history, (B) whether the specific payment month (March) is named, (C) whether the 10% price increase / “this month” timing is included, and (D) whether the agent is asked to calculate and immediately send the difference. Each level gradually restores those details so that all levels still point the agent to inspect recent transactions (the required first tool call). The original prompt (which names March and the 10% increase and asks to send the difference) best matches Level 3.

levels.description: Sparse trigger: mentions Spotify and a price increase plus an action to resolve by looking at transactions; omits month, percent, and calculation details. Still points to checking transaction history first.

Restores the 10% increase and timing wording (this month) and requests the difference for March — matches the original prompt’s level of detail but still leaves rounding/year/recipient specifics unspecified.
must_include: Spotify sent me a note that their prices increased 10% this month

natural_levels.N1: Spotify said they raised their prices. My payment looks too low — please check my recent transaction history and send Spotify whatever amount is missing.
natural_levels.N2: Spotify sent me a note that their prices increased 10% this month. Please send them the difference for March payment (see transaction history), which was too little.

N1.reasoning: The prompt clearly preserves the correct first tool family — inspect recent transaction history — and the intended follow-up (send Spotify the missing amount). However it omits many operational details needed after that first step: which transactions/timeframe count as “recent,” how to identify the Spotify charge (exact merchant identifier), what the new price should be (so the difference can be computed), currency/amount rounding rules, recipient/payment account details, and metadata for the payment (date/subject). These omissions make the request very ambiguous though still workable for an agent that first fetches recent transactions.

N2.reasoning: The prompt clearly indicates the correct first tool: look up the transaction history to find the March payment and then send Spotify the 10% difference. That preserves the right object/source of truth (the March transaction) and the intended action. However it omits many operational details: which March (year), which specific transaction if multiple, recipient banking/identifier to use, exact rounding/currency handling, send date, and any note/subject preferences. These omissions leave meaningful ambiguity for execution even though the first tool path is preserved. Calibration adjusted the score from 2 to 5 based on preserved specificity cues.
